raw_consumer_statement: I returned a watch to Macys that I ordered online on XX/XX/year>. XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX watch. After not receiving a refund, I contacted Macy customer service on XX/XX/year> via web chat. The rep confirmed that the item was received on XX/XX/year>. And that according to their policy I would have a refund in XXXX business days. Also guaranteed that I would get a refund by XX/XX/year>. After not receiving the refund I called Macys on XX/XX/XXXX and it was confirmed the watch was received. The rep stated that due to the amount of the refund {$480.00} it required a special over ride and form. The rep guaranteed I would receive the refund with XXXX XXXX hours. Still no refund. Called Macys back again on XX/XX/year>, the rep stated they would mail me a check. I refused to believe them and escalated to a supervisor. When I spoke to the supervisor they stated that the watch was damaged, so Macys shipped the watch back to me. The rep stated the watch has a scratch on it. That is completely false. I sent the watch back in perfect condition. The supervisor said it was shipped back and I let them know I never received it. The rep said they would file a claim and would have a resolution, and within 48 they would call me back. XX/XX/year> I havent heard back from Macys. So I called Macys back. The rep that I spoke to had no idea about the refund or the claim that was supposedly filed.
